prompt: Audit these changes against the blueprint before I let this merge. files/blueprint.md is the spec, files/code-changes.diff is everything the team changed, files/system-design.md is the design we agreed on, and files/plan.md tracks scenario status. I want to know what's actually wrong with it — assume it's broken.
expectations: Maps each chunk of the diff to the scenario it serves and reports coverage as roughly 2 of 5 scenarios implemented, explicitly naming S3, S4, and S5 as unimplemented.
Identifies the usage_analytics module as an orphan change traceable to no scenario and recommends it not be there (scope creep).
Argues, scenario by scenario, why each implementation is unacceptable rather than assessing whether it is good, holding the adversarial stance even where code superficially looks fine.

Cites specific kite-arch-compass principle numbers/names for the violations (e.g. Principle 2 thin edges and Principle 19 async for the inline assembly in the route; Principle 15 idempotency for S3; least-privilege / never-trust-input for the missing S4 ownership check; Principle 16 / Principle 21 for S5's missing failed terminal state).

States the consequence/impact of each major defect and writes at least one concrete failure case as Gherkin (given/when/then) where the implementation breaks (e.g. a retried POST creating duplicate exports, or a cross-workspace export succeeding).
Surfaces at least one corner case the blueprint did not list (e.g. concurrent file mutation during assembly, unbounded memory on large apps, or stale/orphaned export artifacts).
Delivers the findings as a review report for a human to decide on and does not itself fix the code or re-run the implementation/planning pipeline.
